Tests the behavior of the modulus operator when dividing by zero in JavaScript.

# Purpose
This code is a simple test script that uses the `assert` function to verify the behavior of the modulus operator when dividing by zero. It checks that the result of the expression `5 % 0` is `NaN` (Not-a-Number), which is the expected outcome in JavaScript when performing a modulus operation with zero as the divisor. This script provides narrow functionality, focusing on a specific edge case in arithmetic operations.
